overview:
## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if a transition is allowed from the current state to a state of the specified class; otherwise [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Discussion

You build a state machine by creating a unique [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState] subclass for each distinct state possible for the machine. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/isValidNextState(_:)] method of each state object determines which other states a state machine is allowed to transition into from that state.

A newly created state machine’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/currentState] property is `nil`. In this case, the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/canEnterState(_:)] method always returns [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true]. To choose and enter an initial state, use the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/enter(_:)] method.

# canEnterState(_:)

**Instance Method**

Returns a Boolean value indicating whether it is valid for the state machine to transition from its current state to a state of the specified class.

## Declaration

```swift
func canEnterState(_ stateClass: AnyClass) -> Bool
```

## Parameters

- **stateClass**: The class of state for which to determine whether a transition is allowed.

## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if a transition is allowed from the current state to a state of the specified class; otherwise [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Discussion

You build a state machine by creating a unique [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState] subclass for each distinct state possible for the machine. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKState/isValidNextState(_:)] method of each state object determines which other states a state machine is allowed to transition into from that state.

A newly created state machine’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/currentState] property is `nil`. In this case, the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/canEnterState(_:)] method always returns [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true]. To choose and enter an initial state, use the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKStateMachine/enter(_:)] method.

## Working with States

- **currentState**: The state machine’s current state.
- **enter(_:)**: Attempts to transition the state machine from its current state to a state of the specified class.
- **update(deltaTime:)**: Tells the current state object to perform per-frame updates.
